Cs. we, v. _ú-cag ('u cag).
n. of a country Glr.
with lóg-pa (log pa), Sch.: to perform somersets, to tumble over, to roll.
although, Sch.; _a-na-ma-na ('a na ma na) Sch.; perfectly alike, having a striking resemblance (?).
but, e.g. _a-ma ma rjed cig ('a ma ma rjed cig) but do not forget! Cs.
Sch.: 'shaking or rattling sounds' cf. _ur-_úr ('ur 'ur).

1. like yaṅ (yang), attached to conjunctions, and corresponding to the English ever, soever, after vowels, col. also after consonants, e.g. nam-_aṅ (nam 'ang). - 2. _aṅ-sgra ('ang sgra), bón-bui (bon bu'i) Cs. the braying of an ass.
(not ident. with ăṅ-gi (ang gi) number), a mystical character, frq. occurring in certain finical ornaments or flourishes called sbrúl-mgo (sbrul mgo), occasionally also in written words.
Ts. to bark.
Ts. angry = gtúm-po (gtum po).
C. lot, rgyáb-pa (rgyab pa) to cast, = rgyan rgyáb-pa (rgyan rgyab pa).
v. _ur-_úr ('ur 'ur).
also, too, likewise Sch.
1. num. figure: 53. - 2. W. demonstr. pron. inst. of _di ('di), this, also 'i-po ('i po).
1. num. figure: 83. - 2. sbst. kiss, v. _o ('o). - 3. also _o ('o), Cs.: demonstr. pron., this, _ú-ni-ru ('u ni ru), _ú-nir ('u nir), _ó-nir ('o nir), hither; Ts. *wú-ahi* (*wu ahi*) this.
Glr. noise of many foot-steps, prob. = _úr-sgra ('ur sgra).
1. also _ó-cag ('o cag) Glr., _ó-cog ('o cog) Thgy., _ú-bu-cag ('u bu cag) Dzl. pers. pron. we. - 2. chimney W. (?).

Sch.: 'Lüderlichkeit, auch _u-tsúgs ('u tshugs)'; but in W. *'un-tug ċó-ċe* (*'un tug co ce*) means to break out into a violent passion, and *'ún-tug-kan* (*'un tug kan*) or *-can* (*-can*) angry; in C. *mú-tug-pa* (*mu tug pa*) and *dúg-tug-pa* (*dug tug pa*) to be at a loss; so also in Mil.
v. _ú-cag ('u cag).
Sch. = ur (ur).

compulsory post-service, the gratuitous forwarding of letters, luggage and persons, the supply of the requisite porters and beasts of burden (also more immediately these themselves), - originally a socage-service rendered to lords and proprietors, government officers and priests; in more recent times remunerated and legally regulated in those parts that are visited by European travellers; mí-la _u-lág skúl-ba (mi la 'u lag skul ba) to impose such services, by exacting porters etc. Pth., _gél-ba ('gel ba) id.; skyél-ba (skyel ba) prob. to forward by Ulag; (Cs. limits the signification too much).
Lt. coriander seed.

owl, Lt.; _ug-rgán ('ug rgan) Sch. the great horn-owl, _ug(-gu)-cúṅ ('ug (-gu) -cung) the little owl; _ug-míg ('ug mig) owl's eyes (Cs. 'large languishing eyes', Sch.: 'large protruding eyes'); _úg-mig-can ('ug mig can) having such eyes, _úg-mig-pa ('ug mig pa) or -ma (-ma) a goggle-eyed man or woman Cs. - 2. Ld. also for yug-po (yug po) oats.
v. síṅs-po (sings po).

1. Cs. swaggering, bragging, bombast, fustian; _ud cér smra-ba ('ud cer smra ba) to swagger, brag, gen. *wur ṡe-pa* (*wur she pa*), C. - 2. = yud (yud) Thgy., _ud-kyís ('ud kyis), in a moment, instantly, suddenly. - 3. command, order (?), Sch.: _ud-sgrog-pa ('ud sgrog pa) to make known an order.
to sweep or rake together with one's hands, paṅ-pas _ub-kyis bsdus-te (pang pas 'ub kyis bsdus te) Pth. with the arms gathering all into one heap.
n. of the palace of the ancient Tibetan king Thothori, Glr.
